Born Haven, Charles Goodyear attended school Naugatuck and, 1826, started first retail domestic hardware store with father, inventor businessman Amasa Goodyear, Philadelphia.
Bankrupt four years later, Charles family endured poverty doggedly pursued venture: stabilization rubber commercial use.
1844, after establishing Naugatuck India-Rubber Company, Goodyear patented vulcanization process discovery that made possible range indispensible commercial products, from tires sneaker treads.
Despite this success, Goodyear battled patent infringements debt until death 1860. buried Haven inducted into National Inventors Hall Fame 1976.
